verses.latin_vulgate: Confractus est Bel, contritus est Nabo ;  facta sunt simulacra eorum bestiis et jumentis,  onera vestra gravi pondere  usque ad lassitudinem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כָּרַ֥ע בֵּל֙ קֹרֵ֣ס נְב֔וֹ הָיוּ֙ עֲצַבֵּיהֶ֔ם לַחַיָּ֖ה וְלַבְּהֵמָ֑ה נְשֻׂאֹתֵיכֶ֣ם עֲמוּס֔וֹת מַשָּׂ֖א לַעֲיֵפָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Bel boweth down, Nebo stoopeth, their idols were upon the beasts, and upon the cattle: your carriages were heavy loaden; they are a burden to the weary beast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contabuerunt, et contrita sunt simul ;  non potuerunt salvare portantem,  et anima eorum in captivitatem ibit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קָרְס֤וּ כָֽרְעוּ֙ יַחְדָּ֔ו לֹ֥א יָכְל֖וּ מַלֵּ֣ט מַשָּׂ֑א וְנַפְשָׁ֖ם בַּשְּׁבִ֥י הָלָֽכָה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: They stoop, they bow down together; they could not deliver the burden, but themselves are gone into captivity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite me, domus Jacob,  et omne residuum domus Israël ;  qui portamini a meo utero,  qui gestamini a mea vulva.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֤וּ אֵלַי֙ בֵּ֣ית יַעֲקֹ֔ב וְכׇל־שְׁאֵרִ֖ית בֵּ֣ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל הַֽעֲמֻסִים֙ מִנִּי־בֶ֔טֶן הַנְּשֻׂאִ֖ים מִנִּי־רָֽחַם׃
verses.king_james: Hearken unto me, O house of Jacob, and all the remnant of the house of Israel, which are borne by me from the belly, which are carried from the womb:

verses.latin_vulgate: Usque ad senectam ego ipse,  et usque ad canos ego portabo ;  ego feci, et ego feram ;  ego portabo, et salvabo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַד־זִקְנָה֙ אֲנִ֣י ה֔וּא וְעַד־שֵׂיבָ֖ה אֲנִ֣י אֶסְבֹּ֑ל אֲנִ֤י עָשִׂ֙יתִי֙ וַאֲנִ֣י אֶשָּׂ֔א וַאֲנִ֥י אֶסְבֹּ֖ל וַאֲמַלֵּֽט׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And even to your old age I am he; and even to hoar hairs will I carry you: I have made, and I will bear; even I will carry, and will deliver you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui assimilastis me, et adæquastis,  et comparastis me, et fecistis similem ?
verses.king_james: To whom will ye liken me, and make me equal, and compare me, that we may be like?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui confertis aurum de sacculo,  et argentum statera ponderatis,  conducentes aurificem ut faciat deum,  et procidunt, et adorant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַזָּלִ֤ים זָהָב֙ מִכִּ֔יס וְכֶ֖סֶף בַּקָּנֶ֣ה יִשְׁקֹ֑לוּ יִשְׂכְּר֤וּ צוֹרֵף֙ וְיַעֲשֵׂ֣הוּ אֵ֔ל יִסְגְּד֖וּ אַף־יִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: They lavish gold out of the bag, and weigh silver in the balance, and hire a goldsmith; and he maketh it a god: they fall down, yea, they worship.

verses.latin_vulgate: Portant illum in humeris gestantes,  et ponentes in loco suo,  et stabit, ac de loco suo non movebitur :  sed et cum clamaverint ad eum, non audiet ;  de tribulatione non salvabit eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֠שָּׂאֻ֠הוּ עַל־כָּתֵ֨ף יִסְבְּלֻ֜הוּ וְיַנִּיחֻ֤הוּ תַחְתָּיו֙ וְיַֽעֲמֹ֔ד מִמְּקוֹמ֖וֹ לֹ֣א יָמִ֑ישׁ אַף־יִצְעַ֤ק אֵלָיו֙ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲנֶ֔ה מִצָּרָת֖וֹ לֹ֥א יוֹשִׁיעֶֽנּוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: They bear him upon the shoulder, they carry him, and set him in his place, and he standeth; from his place shall he not remove: yea, one shall cry unto him, yet can he not answer, nor save him out of his trouble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mementote istud, et confundamini ;  redite, prævaricatores, ad cor.
verses.king_james: Remember this, and shew yourselves men: bring it again to mind, O ye transgressors.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordamini prioris sæculi,  quoniam ego sum Deus, et non est ultra deus,  nec est similis mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זִכְר֥וּ רִאשֹׁנ֖וֹת מֵעוֹלָ֑ם כִּ֣י אָנֹכִ֥י אֵל֙ וְאֵ֣ין ע֔וֹד אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְאֶ֥פֶס כָּמֽוֹנִי׃
verses.king_james: Remember the former things of old: for I am God, and there is none else; I am God, and there is none like me,

verses.latin_vulgate: Annuntians ab exordio novissimum,  et ab initio quæ necdum facta sunt,  dicens : Consilium meum stabit,  et omnis voluntas mea fiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַגִּ֤יד מֵֽרֵאשִׁית֙ אַחֲרִ֔ית וּמִקֶּ֖דֶם אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹא־נַֽעֲשׂ֑וּ אֹמֵר֙ עֲצָתִ֣י תָק֔וּם וְכׇל־חֶפְצִ֖י אֶעֱשֶֽׂה׃
verses.king_james: Declaring the end from the beginning, and from ancient times the things that are not yet done, saying, My counsel shall stand, and I will do all my pleasure:

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocans ab oriente avem,  et de terra longinqua virum voluntatis meæ :  et locutus sum, et adducam illud ;  creavi et faciam illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֹרֵ֤א מִמִּזְרָח֙ עַ֔יִט מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מֶרְחָ֖ק אִ֣ישׁ (עצתו) [עֲצָתִ֑י] אַף־דִּבַּ֙רְתִּי֙ אַף־אֲבִיאֶ֔נָּה יָצַ֖רְתִּי אַף־אֶעֱשֶֽׂנָּה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Calling a ravenous bird from the east, the man that executeth my counsel from a far country: yea, I have spoken it, I will also bring it to pass; I have purposed it, I will also do it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite me, duro corde,  qui longe estis a justitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֥וּ אֵלַ֖י אַבִּ֣ירֵי לֵ֑ב הָרְחוֹקִ֖ים מִצְּדָקָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Hearken unto me, ye stouthearted, that are far from righteousness:

verses.latin_vulgate: Prope feci justitiam meam, non elongabitur,  et salus mea non morabitur.  Dabo in Sion salutem,  et in Israël gloriam meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֵרַ֤בְתִּי צִדְקָתִי֙ לֹ֣א תִרְחָ֔ק וּתְשׁוּעָתִ֖י לֹ֣א תְאַחֵ֑ר וְנָתַתִּ֤י בְצִיּוֹן֙ תְּשׁוּעָ֔ה לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל תִּפְאַרְתִּֽי׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: I bring near my righteousness; it shall not be far off, and my salvation shall not tarry: and I will place salvation in Zion for Israel my glory.
